name: Macarons de Saint-Emilion
summary: An easy Saint-Emilion snack and edible souvenir tied to the village's historic almond macaron tradition.
whyItMatters: This is the low-friction food memory for Saint-Emilion: quick, local, portable, and open daily under official hours.
pros: Directly tied to Saint-Emilion's historic specialty
cons: Could be busy with village tourism

logistics.takeaway_options: Excellent; shop sells macarons and boxes to carry.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging/day plan is confirmed; in central Saint-Emilion village.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not a race-day viewing food option.
logistics.transit: Walkable within Saint-Emilion village; verify broader train/taxi plan from Bordeaux.
logistics.parking: Unknown; village parking may require walking.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No Tour de France closure relevance found for this non-viewing day candidate.

timing.best_visit_window: Any Saint-Emilion village walk; buy before leaving town.
timing.race_day_risk: Low if not tied to race-day transfer timing.

sources.title: Macarons de Saint-Emilion official website
sources.note: Supports address, daily hours, takeaway/boutique role, ingredients, and the 1620 recipe/history claim.

open_questions: Confirm whether the group wants a small food-souvenir stop built into the Saint-Emilion walk.
